source: Telegram UX review 2026-06-21 — Pi cannot use native chat affordances (reactions, inline buttons, editing its own messages) that make a bot feel intuitive rather than a text pipe.
title: Telegram-native actions: reactions, inline buttons, self-edit
current_state: Pi can only send text/media and reply-to. It cannot react to a message with an emoji, offer quick-reply inline-keyboard buttons, or edit a message it already sent. The experience is one-directional text; the chat-native affordances Telegram offers go unused.
target_state: Pi (via the LLM tool loop) can, when on Telegram: (a) react to the user's message with an emoji (setMessageReaction), (b) attach inline-keyboard quick-reply buttons to a reply (reply_markup) and handle the resulting callback, (c) edit its own last message (editMessageText) for corrections/progressive detail. Exposed as Telegram-only tools, no-ops/declined on other channels.
migration_plan: 1. Expose current Telegram message context (bot instance, chat_id, message_id) to handlers so action tools can target the right message — a per-turn context object set during dispatch.

2. New ToolSpecs in tools_telegram.py TOOLS: telegram_react(emoji), telegram_buttons(text, options[]), telegram_edit_last(text). Each guards on channel=='telegram' (from T-218) and returns a clear declined result elsewhere.
3. Register a callback_query handler for inline-button presses; route the chosen value back through process_input as a normal turn.
4. Update the channel-awareness block (T-218) to tell Pi these actions exist and when to use them (sparingly).

5. Tests: new testing/test_telegram_native_actions.py — react/edit/buttons call the right bot method with right args; tools decline cleanly off-Telegram; callback press routes back through process_input.
risk_notes: Highest-effort, lowest-urgency — the delight layer. Build only after T-217/T-218/T-219 land the foundation. Keep actions opt-in and rare in the prompt guidance so Pi doesn't spam reactions/buttons. setMessageReaction requires a recent pyTelegramBotAPI — verify the installed telebot version supports it before relying on it.
